prompt:
Create a vertical travel poster illustration of SAKAE, NAGOYA in a refined watercolor-and-ink urban sketch style on warm off-white textured paper. The scene shows a lively street intersection in Sakae, Nagoya, Japan, viewed from sidewalk level with strong depth perspective. On the left foreground, draw a charming old corner cafe building with weathered cream walls, black awning, large window, warm interior seating, potted plants, climbing leafy vines, and a parked green bicycle leaning near the curb; include readable cafe signage: “KAKUOZAN LARDER”, “BLEND”, and “COFFEE”. Place a small chalkboard sign outside reading “COFFEE / SANDWICH / and / GOOD MUSIC” with a tiny cup drawing. In the center distance, feature the Nagoya TV Tower rising clearly above trees and city buildings. On the right, show a modern elevated glass-and-metal transit structure, streetlights, traffic signals, a “Sakae” sign, road markings, bicycles, and pedestrians crossing a broad zebra crossing. Include one prominent woman seen from behind in the lower center foreground wearing a light beige long coat and carrying a brown shoulder bag, walking into the city. Add many small pedestrians but keep them sketchy and atmospheric. Use delicate black pen linework, pale gray shadows, muted blue-gray architecture, soft green foliage, and restrained watercolor washes with lots of white space.

Poster typography: At the upper right, set the large bracketed headline “[SAKAE, NAGOYA]” in elegant tall serif capitals. Beneath it, add the handwritten script subtitle “Everyday Urban Rhythm”, a thin horizontal rule, and small uppercase text “SAKAE, NAKA WARD, NAGOYA, JAPAN”. Farther right, add a compact quote block: “A CITY THAT BLENDS / TRADITION AND INNOVATION, / PEOPLE AND CULTURE, / IN PERFECT HARMONY.” In the bottom left margin, add a small circular travel stamp reading “NAGOYA / 名古屋 / JAPAN”. Keep the composition airy, sophisticated, editorial, and print-ready, with no photo-realism and no extra text.
